name: Leeds Teaching Hospitals NHS Trust
description: First hospital in the UK to go 100% digital for primary histopathology diagnosis (2018). Leads the National Pathology Imaging Co-operative (NPIC).

name: Charité – Universitätsmedizin Berlin
description: One of the largest and most prestigious university hospitals in Europe, with a renowned Institute of Pathology.

name: AP-HP (Assistance Publique – Hôpitaux de Paris)
description: Largest public hospital group in Europe, comprising 39 hospitals across the Paris region with extensive anatomical pathology services.

name: King's College Hospital
description: Major academic teaching hospital in south London with a large pathology department and strong research output.

name: UZ Leuven
description: Largest university hospital in Belgium. Signed a 10-year contract with Sectra to fully digitize anatomical pathology workflows.

name: UMC Utrecht
description: Fully digital pathology department using Sectra Digital Pathology with 4× Hamamatsu NanoZoomer scanners processing approximately 800 slides per day.

name: San Raffaele Hospital
description: Major Italian research hospital and IRCCS with a strong anatomical pathology department integrated into its clinical and translational research programs.

name: Vall d'Hebron University Hospital
description: Fully digital via the DigiPatICS initiative covering 8 Catalan hospitals, processing over 1 million slides per year.

name: Karolinska University Hospital
description: Sweden's largest university hospital and a leading European center for clinical research, with extensive pathology and laboratory medicine services.

name: Haukeland University Hospital
description: Western Norway's largest hospital and a key node in the European Society for Digital and Integrative Pathology (ESDIP) digital map.

name: University of Helsinki / HUS Diagnostic Center
description: Finland's leading academic pathology department within the Helsinki University Hospital diagnostic center.

name: Odense University Hospital
description: Major Danish university hospital and part of the European digital pathology research network.

name: Erasmus MC
description: Largest academic medical center in the Netherlands with a prominent department of pathology.

name: Antoni van Leeuwenhoek Hospital (Netherlands Cancer Institute)
description: Dedicated comprehensive cancer center and research institute with specialized surgical pathology and molecular diagnostics.

name: CHU Montpellier
description: Major southern French teaching hospital listed on the ESDIP digital pathology map as an adopter of digital workflows.

name: Hospital Universitario de Burgos
description: Spanish university hospital featured on the ESDIP digital pathology map, actively pursuing digitization of pathology services.

name: Centre Hospitalier de Versailles
description: Public hospital near Paris listed on the ESDIP digital pathology map as a participant in digital pathology adoption.

name: Medical University of Vienna (AKH Wien)
description: Austria's largest medical university and hospital, housing the Clinical Institute of Pathology with a broad diagnostic and research portfolio.

name: University Hospital Zurich (USZ)
description: Switzerland's largest university hospital with a renowned Department of Pathology and Molecular Pathology.

name: Fondazione Policlinico Universitario Agostino Gemelli
description: One of Italy's largest teaching hospitals, affiliated with the Catholic University of the Sacred Heart, with an extensive anatomical pathology unit.

name: SYNLAB Germany (Headquarters)
description: European headquarters of SYNLAB, the largest European medical diagnostics provider with 1,800+ sites across 36 countries.

name: SYNLAB Belgium (Anatomical Pathology Center)
description: SYNLAB's Belgian anatomical pathology center serving multiple hospitals and clinics in the Brussels region.

name: Sonic Healthcare Germany (Headquarters)
description: German operations of Sonic Healthcare, operating 79+ pathology and laboratory medicine practices across Germany.

name: Sonic Healthcare UK (The Halo Lab)
description: One of Europe's largest pathology laboratories, part of Sonic Healthcare's UK operations providing comprehensive histopathology services.

name: Cerba Path Central Lab
description: Central anatomical pathology laboratory of Cerba HealthCare employing 44 pathologists across its French network.

name: Eurofins Biomnis
description: Flagship specialized testing laboratory of the Eurofins network, one of France's largest reference laboratories for anatomical pathology and specialized diagnostics.

name: Unilabs Switzerland (Headquarters)
description: Headquarters of Unilabs, a major European diagnostics group operating 200+ laboratories in 14 countries.

name: Medicover Germany
description: German hub of Medicover Diagnostics, operating 135 laboratories across Central and Eastern Europe with growing pathology services.

name: Hospital Universitario La Paz
description: One of Spain's largest academic hospitals with a comprehensive anatomical pathology department serving the northern Madrid health area.

name: Bellvitge University Hospital
description: Part of the DigiPatICS digital pathology network, one of eight Catalan hospitals fully digitized for anatomical pathology.

name: Rigshospitalet
description: Denmark's largest and most specialized hospital, affiliated with the University of Copenhagen, with extensive pathology services.

name: Oslo University Hospital
description: Norway's largest hospital and a major European research center with a comprehensive department of pathology.

name: Sahlgrenska University Hospital
description: One of Sweden's largest hospitals and Northern Europe's biggest emergency hospitals, with a substantial clinical pathology department.

name: Uppsala University Hospital
description: Major Swedish academic hospital with a long tradition of pathology research, affiliated with Uppsala University.

name: Medical University of Graz
description: Austria's second-largest medical university with the Diagnostic and Research Institute of Pathology focusing on translational research.

name: CHUV (Centre Hospitalier Universitaire Vaudois)
description: One of Switzerland's five university hospitals with an Institute of Pathology active in both diagnostic and research pathology.

name: Coventry & Warwickshire Pathology Services (UHCW)
description: NHS pathology services for the Coventry and Warwickshire region, featured on the ESDIP digital pathology map.

name: PALGA (Dutch Pathology Registry)
description: National network hub connecting all 43 Dutch pathology laboratories. Maintains the nationwide pathology database with over 2 million cases per year.

name: University Hospitals Birmingham NHS Foundation Trust
description: Queen Elizabeth Hospital Birmingham. One of the largest NHS trusts in England with a UKAS-accredited cellular pathology department and major cancer diagnostics centre.

name: Manchester University NHS Foundation Trust
description: Operates the Greater Manchester Cellular Pathology Genomics Centre across multiple sites including Oxford Road, Wythenshawe, and The Christie. Major hub for cancer genomics.

name: Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust
description: Histopathology services at City Hospital and QMC campuses. Key provider in the East Midlands pathology network.

name: Newcastle upon Tyne Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust
description: One of the UK's largest cellular pathology services based at the Royal Victoria Infirmary. Specialist referral centre with strong research links to Newcastle University.

name: Barts Health NHS Trust
description: The Royal London Hospital. Largest NHS trust in England. Part of East and South East London Pathology Partners (ESELPP) providing cellular pathology across East London.

name: Guy's and St Thomas' NHS Foundation Trust
description: Major London teaching hospital. Histopathology services delivered through Synnovis partnership. National/international referral centre for cancer diagnostics in the SE London Cancer Network.

name: Synnovis
description: Pathology partnership between Guy's & St Thomas', King's College Hospital, and SYNLAB. Serves ~2 million patients in SE London. Building a new central hub at Blackfriars Road (2026).

name: Imperial College Healthcare NHS Trust
description: Part of North West London Pathology (NWLP) network serving Imperial, Chelsea & Westminster, and Hillingdon trusts. Academic pathology linked with Imperial College London.

name: North Bristol NHS Trust — Severn Pathology
description: Joint cellular pathology laboratory with University Hospitals Bristol and Weston. ISO 15189:2022 accredited. Serves the greater Bristol and South West region.

name: University Hospitals Plymouth NHS Trust
description: Cellular and anatomical pathology at Derriford Hospital. Major hospital serving Devon and Cornwall.

name: The Royal Marsden NHS Foundation Trust
description: International centre of excellence for cancer diagnostics. Specialist cancer hospital with dedicated cellular pathology department and cutting-edge molecular diagnostics.

name: Cambridge University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust
description: Addenbrooke's Hospital. Central hub for histopathology training in the East of England. Strong research ties with the University of Cambridge.

name: Norfolk and Norwich University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust
description: Processes ~68,000 specimens per year. Covers diagnostics for NNUH, James Paget University Hospital, and local GPs. Regional referral centre.

name: University Hospital Southampton NHS Foundation Trust
description: Cellular Pathology Department with regional specialist services in neuropathology and paediatric/perinatal pathology. Integrated with the University of Southampton.

name: Oxford University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust
description: John Radcliffe Hospital. Major academic histopathology centre with strong research links to the University of Oxford. National leader in clinical research and teaching.

name: Sheffield Teaching Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust
description: Royal Hallamshire Hospital. Hosts the National Specialist Ophthalmic Pathology Service. Major centre for diagnostics, research, and education.

name: Liverpool University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust
description: Liverpool Clinical Laboratories cellular pathology department at Royal Liverpool University Hospital. Specialist services in GI, head & neck, breast, and ophthalmic pathology.

name: University Hospitals of Leicester NHS Trust
description: Cellular pathology at Leicester Royal Infirmary. Processes 60,000+ tissue samples and 5,000 cytology samples annually. UKAS accredited (ISO 15189). East Midlands training centre.

name: Royal Surrey NHS Foundation Trust
description: Cellular pathology closely linked with St. Luke's Cancer Centre. Active in clinical trials and academic research.

name: Hull University Teaching Hospitals NHS Trust
description: Part of the Scarborough, Hull and York Pathology Service (SHYPS). Hosts the cellular pathology referral centres directory for the region.

name: Bradford Teaching Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust
description: Part of the West Yorkshire Association of Acute Trusts (WYAAT) pathology network. Provides cellular pathology for the Bradford district.

name: NHS Lothian — Royal Infirmary of Edinburgh
description: Edinburgh and Lothians Laboratory Medicine. Comprehensive histopathology and molecular pathology across the Royal Infirmary and Western General Hospital. Part of the Scottish Molecular Pathology Consortium.

name: NHS Greater Glasgow and Clyde — Queen Elizabeth University Hospital
description: Scotland's largest health board. Major histopathology training centre. Centralises national cervical cytopathology training and provides substantial molecular diagnostics.

name: NHS Grampian — Aberdeen Royal Infirmary
description: Regional pathology centre with particular strength in respiratory pathology and genomic medicine. One of four nodes in Scotland's molecular pathology network (SSNGM).

name: NHS Tayside — Ninewells Hospital
description: Major pathology centre for Tayside. Strong collaboration with the University of Dundee and St Andrews. Noted for research and teaching in advanced molecular techniques.

name: Cardiff and Vale University Health Board — University Hospital of Wales
description: UKAS accredited to ISO 15189:2022. Major training centre for histopathology in Wales. Services include special stains, immunohistochemistry, and neuropathology diagnostics.

name: Swansea Bay University Health Board — Morriston Hospital
description: Pathology labs at Morriston, Singleton, and Princess of Wales hospitals. Provides cellular pathology and mortuary services for the Swansea Bay region.

name: Betsi Cadwaladr University Health Board — Glan Clwyd Hospital
description: Primary histopathology laboratory for North Wales. Collaborates with Liverpool for specialist training and tertiary referrals.

name: Belfast Health and Social Care Trust
description: Leading UK centre for digital pathology. Tissue Pathology and Molecular Sciences (TPMS) division. Regional molecular diagnostics service and strong academic partnership with Queen's University Belfast Precision Medicine Centre.

name: Health Services Laboratories (HSL)
description: Joint venture between UCLH, Royal Free London, and Sonic Healthcare. Hub for cellular pathology serving UCLH, Whittington, Royal Free, North Middlesex, and Royal Brompton hospitals.

name: The Doctors Laboratory (TDL)
description: One of the UK's largest independent medical laboratories. Subspecialty histopathology including GI, dermatological, breast, urological, and respiratory pathology. ISO 15189 accredited, CQC regulated. 24/7 operations.

name: BUPA Cromwell Hospital — Pathology Department
description: Private hospital with pathology services operated by TDL. Provides full clinical and histopathology services for private patients in West London.

name: St James's Hospital Dublin
description: One of the largest histology laboratories in Ireland. Comprehensive diagnostics including FISH, automated IHC, and molecular diagnostics. Subspecialties in breast, lung, GI, head & neck, and haematological pathology.

name: Beaumont Hospital Dublin
description: Designated cancer centre handling tertiary referrals in neurosciences and renal transplantation pathology. INAB accredited. Strong academic collaboration with RCSI, TCD, UCD, and DCU.

name: Mater Misericordiae University Hospital Dublin
description: Local, regional, and national diagnostic pathology services. INAB accredited to ISO 15189. Supports BreastCheck screening programme and specialised cancer diagnostics.

name: St Vincent's University Hospital Dublin
description: Full pathology and laboratory medicine suite. Regional/national referral centre for specialised tests. INAB accredited. Detailed Pathology User Handbook available for GPs.

name: Cork University Hospital
description: Department of Pathology in the UCC Clinical Sciences Building. INAB accredited laboratory medicine. Close academic ties with University College Cork.

name: University Hospital Galway
description: Division of Anatomic Pathology. Leading centre for cancer diagnostics in Western Ireland. Active research in breast, prostate, and colon cancer. Digital pathology and biobanking champion Prof Seán Hynes.

name: University Hospital Limerick
description: Regional hospital serving the Mid-West of Ireland. Histopathology department supports cancer diagnostics and general surgical pathology for the UL Hospitals Group.

name: Tallaght University Hospital Dublin
description: Major academic teaching hospital affiliated with Trinity College Dublin. Provides histopathology and laboratory medicine for south-west Dublin and surrounding counties.

name: University Hospitals Coventry and Warwickshire NHS Trust
description: University Hospital Coventry. Part of the Coventry and Warwickshire Pathology Services (CWPS). Academic links with Warwick Medical School.

name: Universitätsklinikum Heidelberg – Pathologisches Institut
description: One of Germany's leading academic pathology institutes, closely linked to DKFZ and NCT Heidelberg for translational cancer research.

name: LMU Klinikum München – Pathologisches Institut
description: Major academic pathology department at Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität, one of the largest in Bavaria.

name: TU München – Institut für Allgemeine Pathologie und Pathologische Anatomie
description: Pathology institute at Klinikum rechts der Isar, Technische Universität München, with strong oncology focus.

name: Universitätsklinikum Hamburg-Eppendorf (UKE) – Institut für Pathologie
description: One of the largest university pathology departments in Germany, processing over 75,000 cases per year with broad subspecialty expertise.

name: Uniklinik Köln – Institut für Pathologie
description: Large academic pathology center with strong oncology focus, certified reference center and nNGM partner.

name: Goethe-Universität Frankfurt – Senckenbergisches Institut für Pathologie
description: Historic Senckenberg Institute of Pathology, renowned for academic research and diagnostic services.

name: Universitätsklinikum Freiburg – Institut für Klinische Pathologie
description: Academic pathology institute at one of Germany's top-ranked university hospitals.

name: Universitätsklinikum Tübingen – Institut für Pathologie und Neuropathologie
description: Leading pathology and neuropathology institute with comprehensive diagnostic and research programs.

name: Universitätsmedizin Göttingen – Institut für Pathologie
description: Pioneer in fully digital pathology in Germany, with AI-assisted diagnostics and digital biopsy workflows. National leader in digitization.

name: Universitätsklinikum Münster – Gerhard-Domagk-Institut für Pathologie
description: Named after Nobel laureate Gerhard Domagk, offering modern histological, molecular and digital pathology services.

name: Universitätsklinikum Essen – Institut für Pathologie
description: Academic pathology institute within the West German Cancer Center, with digital microscopy innovation (SmartZoom ClassRoom).

name: Universitätsklinikum Dresden – Institut für Pathologie
description: Pathology institute at Carl Gustav Carus university hospital with focus on tumor biology and molecular diagnostics.

name: Universitätsklinikum Leipzig – Institut für Pathologie
description: Academic pathology institute providing diagnostics for the UKL and regional hospitals.

name: Universitätsklinikum Bonn – Institut für Pathologie
description: Internationally recognized academic pathology department at University Hospital Bonn.

name: Universitätsklinikum Erlangen – Pathologisches Institut
description: Academic pathology institute of Friedrich-Alexander-Universität Erlangen-Nürnberg.

name: Universitätsmedizin Mainz – Institut für Pathologie
description: Academic pathology institute at Johannes Gutenberg University Mainz with broad diagnostic spectrum.

name: Universitätsklinikum Düsseldorf – Institut für Pathologie
description: Leading academic pathology institute focusing on personalized medicine, involved in national genomic sequencing projects.

name: Medizinische Hochschule Hannover (MHH) – Institut für Pathologie
description: Academic pathology institute at MHH with dedicated molecular pathology professorship and NGS-based diagnostics.

name: Uniklinik RWTH Aachen – Institut für Pathologie
description: Academic pathology institute recognized for digital pathology research and as nNGM partner for lung pathology.

name: Universitätsklinikum Würzburg – Institut für Pathologie
description: Academic pathology institute at Julius-Maximilians-Universität Würzburg.

name: Universitätsklinikum Jena – Sektion Pathologie
description: Pathology section at UKJ with particular expertise in soft tissue tumors and accredited diagnostic services.

name: Universitätsmedizin Rostock – Institut für Pathologie
description: Academic pathology institute with focus on cancer diagnostics and intraoperative frozen section analysis.

name: Universitätsmedizin Greifswald – Institut für Pathologie
description: Academic pathology institute at Universitätsmedizin Greifswald serving northeastern Germany.

name: Universitätsklinikum Magdeburg – Institut für Pathologie
description: Academic pathology institute at Otto von Guericke University Magdeburg.

name: UKSH Kiel – Institut für Pathologie
description: Part of UKSH Schleswig-Holstein, one of the largest academic medical centers in northern Germany.

name: UKSH Lübeck – Institut für Pathologie
description: Second campus of UKSH providing pathology services for the Lübeck region.

name: SYNLAB MVZ Pathologie Hannover
description: SYNLAB pathology lab in Hannover offering morphological and molecular diagnostics for clinics and practices.

name: SYNLAB Pathologie Mannheim
description: SYNLAB pathology site in Mannheim providing comprehensive tissue and molecular diagnostics.

name: SYNLAB Pathologie Mutlangen
description: SYNLAB pathology laboratory in Mutlangen (Baden-Württemberg) serving regional clinics and practices.

name: SYNLAB Institut für Pathologie und Molekularpathologie Pforzheim
description: SYNLAB pathology and molecular pathology institute in Pforzheim with 24h biopsy turnaround times.

name: SYNLAB MVZ Pathologie Trier
description: SYNLAB pathology site in Trier providing diagnostic services for the Rhineland-Palatinate region.

name: SYNLAB MVZ Pathologie Augsburg
description: SYNLAB pathology laboratory in Augsburg, part of the nationwide SYNLAB pathology network.

name: Bioscientia Institut für Medizinische Diagnostik – Ingelheim
description: Headquarters of Bioscientia (Sonic Healthcare Germany), one of Germany's largest diagnostic providers with pathology services.

name: MVZ Prof. Dr. med. Niendorf Pathologie Hamburg-West
description: One of the largest private pathology practices in northern Germany, handling 85,000+ histology and 250,000+ cytology cases annually.

name: OptiPath – MVZ für Pathologie Frankfurt
description: Physician-led pathology MVZ in Frankfurt with DIN EN ISO/IEC 17020 accreditation and broad diagnostic expertise.

name: Pathodiagnostik Berlin MVZ GmbH
description: Private pathology practice in Berlin specializing in hematopathology, uropathology and advanced immunohistochemistry.

name: Zentrum für Pathologie und Zytodiagnostik (ZPZ) Köln
description: Private pathology and cytodiagnostics center in Cologne serving hospitals and practices in NRW.

name: MVZ für Pathologie Heidelberg
description: Private pathology MVZ in Heidelberg led by experienced pathologists, serving outpatient physicians.

name: MVZ Labor Dr. Limbach & Kollegen – Heidelberg
description: One of Germany's largest independent lab groups with over 40 years of operation, providing pathology and laboratory diagnostics.

name: amedes MVZ Hamburg
description: Part of the nationwide amedes group providing laboratory and diagnostic services in Hamburg.

name: amedes MVZ wagnerstibbe Göttingen
description: One of the largest medical diagnostic providers in the Göttingen region, part of the amedes interdisciplinary network.

name: Medizinische Universität Innsbruck – Institut für Pathologie
description: Institute for Pathology, Neuropathology and Molecular Pathology at Medizinische Universität Innsbruck with advanced diagnostic services.

name: Kepler Universitätsklinikum Linz – Klinisches Institut für Pathologie und Molekularpathologie
description: Clinical institute combining diagnostics with academic research at JKU/Kepler University Hospital, with focus on digital pathology.

name: Universitätsinstitut für Pathologie – Landeskrankenhaus Salzburg (SALK)
description: ISO-accredited university pathology institute at SALK with focus on tumor diagnostics and tumor board support.

name: Universitätsspital Basel – Institut für Pathologie
description: Pathology institute at Universitätsspital Basel with strong translational research in cancer and neuroscience.

name: Inselspital Bern – Institut für Tissue Medicine and Pathology
description: Academic pathology institute at Inselspital/University of Bern with integrated Zentrum für Labormedizin and biobank platforms.

name: Kantonsspital St. Gallen – Institut für Pathologie
description: Pathology institute at Kantonsspital St. Gallen, a major teaching hospital in eastern Switzerland.

name: Hôpitaux Universitaires de Genève (HUG) – Département de Médecine Génétique, de Laboratoire et de Pathologie
description: ISO 15189-accredited department integrating pathology, genetics and laboratory medicine at Geneva's university hospital.

name: CHU Lille – Centre de Biologie Pathologie
description: Major CHU in northern France with the Centre de Biologie Pathologie Pierre-Marie Degand. Strong expertise in oncologic and molecular diagnostics.

name: CHU de Bordeaux – Service de Pathologie
description: Large academic hospital group with pathology services at Hôpital Haut-Lévêque and Pellegrin. National reference center for dermatopathology.

name: Hôpitaux Universitaires de Strasbourg – Département de Pathologie
description: University hospital pathology department at Hôpital de Hautepierre. Key academic pathology center in the Grand Est region.

name: CHU de Nantes – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: Academic pathology department at Hôtel-Dieu providing comprehensive diagnostic histopathology and cytopathology for western France.

name: CHU de Toulouse – Anatomie Cytologie Pathologique
description: Major academic pathology lab at Hôpital Purpan serving the Occitanie region. Active in molecular diagnostics and cancer research.

name: CHU de Nice – Laboratoire Central d'Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: Central pathology laboratory (LCAP) at CHU Nice providing diagnostic and molecular pathology services for the Côte d'Azur region.

name: CHU de Rennes – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: Academic pathology department at Hôpital Pontchaillou with advanced digital pathology systems and molecular genetics capabilities.

name: CHU de Rouen – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: University hospital pathology department in Normandy providing full diagnostic anatomical pathology and cytopathology services.

name: CHU Grenoble Alpes – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: Academic pathology department at CHU Grenoble Alpes serving the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes region with comprehensive histopathology and molecular diagnostics.

name: CHU de Reims – Laboratoire d'Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: Part of the Pôle de Biologie Médicale et Pathologie at CHU Reims providing diagnostic pathology services for the Champagne-Ardenne region.

name: CHU Dijon Bourgogne – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: University hospital pathology department at Hôpital du Bocage providing diagnostic anatomical pathology services for the Burgundy region.

name: CHU Clermont-Ferrand – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: Academic pathology department at CHU Estaing providing histopathology and cytopathology for the Auvergne region.

name: CHU de Poitiers – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: University hospital pathology department serving the Nouvelle-Aquitaine region with comprehensive anatomopathology diagnostics.

name: CHU de Tours – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: Academic pathology department at Hôpital Bretonneau/Trousseau providing diagnostic services for the Centre-Val de Loire region.

name: CHU de Limoges – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: University hospital pathology department serving the Limousin area with full histopathology and cytopathology capabilities.

name: CHRU de Besançon – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: University hospital pathology department at Hôpital Jean Minjoz providing anatomopathology diagnostics for Franche-Comté.

name: CHU de Caen Normandie – Anatomie Pathologique
description: Academic pathology department at Hôpital Côte de Nacre. National reference center for mesothelioma pathology diagnostics.

name: CHU Amiens-Picardie – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: University hospital pathology department in Picardy providing full diagnostic anatomical pathology and cancer diagnostics.

name: CHRU de Nancy – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: Large academic pathology service performing over 335,000 anatomopathology and cytopathology acts per year for the Lorraine region.

name: CHU d'Angers – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: University hospital pathology department providing diagnostic anatomopathology services for the Maine-et-Loire region.

name: CHU de Brest – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: Academic pathology department serving western Brittany with comprehensive histopathology, cytopathology, and molecular diagnostics.

name: CHU de Saint-Étienne – Anatomie et Cytologie Pathologiques
description: University hospital pathology department providing anatomopathology diagnostics for the Loire department and surrounding areas.

name: Institut Curie – Diagnostic and Theranostic Medicine
description: Leading French cancer research and treatment center. The Diagnostic and Theranostic Medicine division integrates anatomical pathology, genetics, and molecular diagnostics at Paris and Saint-Cloud sites.

name: Gustave Roussy – Département de Biologie et Pathologie Médicales
description: Largest cancer center in Europe. The Pôle Pathologie handles tumor identification and characterization, with nationally recognized reference laboratory for neuroblastoma, breast cancer, and sarcomas.

name: Centre Léon Bérard – Département de Biopathologie
description: Major French Comprehensive Cancer Center in Lyon. Biopathology department provides histology, cytology, immunohistochemistry, FISH, CGH, NGS, and PCR for cancer diagnostics and precision oncology.

name: Cerba Path Marseille
description: Cerba Path satellite laboratory in Marseille (Château Gombert), part of the Cerba Path national anatomopathology network with 3,000+ collection points across France.

name: Cerba Path Nice
description: Cerba Path office on Avenue Sainte Marguerite in Nice, providing anatomopathology services for the Côte d'Azur area.

name: Cerba Path Caen (Épron)
description: Cerba Path office in Épron near Caen, serving Normandy as part of the national Cerba Path anatomopathology network.

name: Cerba Path Nîmes
description: Cerba Path office in Nîmes (Rue du Mail), providing anatomopathology services for the Gard department and Occitanie region.

name: Cerba Path Rouen
description: Cerba Path office in Rouen (Rue d'Emendreville), serving upper Normandy as part of the Cerba Path national network.

name: UZ Gent – Laboratory for Cancer Diagnosis and Pathology
description: Major Flemish university hospital. The Laboratory for Cancer Diagnosis and Pathology covers diagnostic surgical pathology, autopsy pathology, and molecular diagnostics. Accredited with ISO 15189.

name: Cliniques Universitaires Saint-Luc – Service d'Anatomie Pathologique
description: Largest hospital in Brussels, affiliated with UCLouvain. Pathology department provides specialized diagnostic services with active clinical trial and translational research programs.

name: CHU de Liège – Service d'Anatomie Pathologique
description: Leading Walloon academic hospital with a comprehensive pathology department offering diagnostic and research services. Participates in Belgian and international multicentric research projects.

name: UZ Antwerpen – Department of Pathological Anatomy
description: University hospital with ISO 15189-accredited pathology service. Collaborates with APARTS (Antwerp Pathology Research and Trial Service) for tissue banking and clinical trial support.

name: UZ Brussel (VUB) – Anatomo-Pathologie
description: University hospital of the Vrije Universiteit Brussel in Jette. Full-spectrum pathology department providing clinical diagnostics, research, and teaching services.

name: AZ Groeninge – Pathologische Anatomie
description: Large regional hospital in West Flanders with a dedicated pathology department specializing in microscopic diagnosis of cell and tissue samples using advanced diagnostic technologies.

name: Amsterdam UMC – Department of Pathology
description: One of the largest academic medical centers in the Netherlands (merger of AMC and VUmc). Major pathology department deeply involved in diagnostics, research, and AI-driven computational pathology.

name: LUMC – Department of Pathology
description: Leiden University Medical Center with a leading pathology department. Fully connected to PALGA national pathology network. Active in translational pathology research.

name: Radboudumc – Department of Pathology
description: Radboud University Medical Center with a renowned pathology department. A global leader in computational pathology and AI-assisted diagnostics, fully integrated into PALGA.

name: UMCG – Department of Pathology and Medical Biology
description: University Medical Center Groningen with a comprehensive pathology department. Key node in the PALGA national system supporting diagnostics and research for northern Netherlands.

name: Maastricht UMC+ – Department of Pathology
description: Academic medical center in southern Netherlands connected to PALGA. Supports clinical and scientific work with digital pathology capabilities and national data sharing.

name: Isala Hospital – Pathology Laboratory
description: One of the largest non-academic teaching hospitals in the Netherlands. Pathology lab connected to PALGA with high accreditation standards and ongoing digital innovation.

name: Catharina Ziekenhuis – Pathology Department
description: Large teaching and referral hospital, member of Santeon network. PALGA-connected pathology lab supporting digital diagnostic workflows with a high-volume cancer center.

name: Centre Hospitalier de Luxembourg – Anatomie Pathologique
description: Main public hospital in Luxembourg with ISO 15189-accredited pathology laboratory. Operates a telepathology platform with the LNS for rapid extemporaneous biopsy diagnosis during surgery.

name: Laboratoire National de Santé – National Center of Pathology
description: National reference laboratory under the Luxembourg Ministry of Health. The National Center of Pathology is its largest department, providing anatomical and molecular pathology, cancer screening (cervical via HPV PCR), and telepathology services.

name: Laboratoires Réunis
description: Largest private laboratory network in Luxembourg with 60+ blood collection centers. ISO 15189-accredited, specializing in molecular diagnostics, genetic testing, and cancer screening programs.

name: Hospital Clínic de Barcelona
description: Top-ranked academic hospital affiliated with the University of Barcelona. Its Department of Pathology is a national reference center for hepatopathology and hematopathology with strong digital pathology adoption.

name: Hospital Universitario 12 de Octubre
description: Major university hospital affiliated with Universidad Complutense de Madrid. One of the largest pathology departments in Spain with extensive subspecialty coverage and research programs.

name: Hospital General Universitario Gregorio Marañón
description: One of Madrid's largest teaching hospitals affiliated with Universidad Complutense. Department of Anatomical Pathology handles high-volume diagnostics and participates in national research networks.

name: Hospital Universitario Ramón y Cajal
description: Major university hospital affiliated with Universidad de Alcalá. Known for its strong pathology research output and molecular diagnostics in oncology, including biomarker studies.

name: Hospital Clínico San Carlos
description: Historic university hospital affiliated with Universidad Complutense de Madrid. Pathology department is one of the oldest in Spain and serves as a teaching reference center.

name: Hospital Universitario Virgen del Rocío
description: The largest hospital complex in Andalusia and one of the biggest in Spain. Affiliated with Universidad de Sevilla, its pathology department serves as a regional reference center for Southern Spain.

name: Hospital Universitari i Politècnic La Fe
description: Leading university hospital in the Valencia region affiliated with Universitat de València. Its pathology department is a major reference center for the Comunitat Valenciana.

name: Hospital Universitario Marqués de Valdecilla
description: Major university hospital in Cantabria affiliated with Universidad de Cantabria. Its pathology department serves the entire Cantabria region and is involved in digital pathology research.

name: Complexo Hospitalario Universitario de Santiago de Compostela
description: Main university hospital of Galicia affiliated with Universidade de Santiago de Compostela. Pathology department is a reference center for northwest Spain.

name: Hospital Universitario de Navarra
description: The main public hospital of the Navarra region. Its pathology department covers comprehensive anatomical pathology services for the region.

name: Hospital Universitari Son Espases
description: The reference university hospital for the Balearic Islands. Its anatomical pathology department provides diagnostic services for the entire island region.

name: Hospital Universitario Miguel Servet
description: Largest hospital in Aragón serving as the main reference center for the region. The pathology department handles high-volume diagnostics and research.

name: Hospital Universitari Germans Trias i Pujol
description: Part of the DigiPatICS network, one of the world's largest fully digital pathology deployments. Over 170 pathologists across the network use AI-assisted analysis on millions of digitized slides annually.

name: Hospital Universitari Arnau de Vilanova
description: Member of the DigiPatICS fully digital pathology network operated by the Catalan Health Institute (ICS). All histopathology slides are digitized with high-precision scanners.

name: Hospital Universitari Doctor Josep Trueta
description: DigiPatICS network hospital in the Girona region. Part of the pioneering Catalan initiative for fully digital pathology integration across public hospitals.

name: Hospital Universitari Joan XXIII
description: Part of the DigiPatICS digital pathology network covering the Tarragona province. Fully digitized histopathology workflow with AI support.

name: Institut Català d'Oncologia (ICO)
description: Catalonia's public cancer center integrated with DigiPatICS hospitals (Bellvitge, Germans Trias, Vall d'Hebron). Specializes in cancer diagnostics and treatment with fully digital pathology workflows.

name: Hospital Universitario Fundación Jiménez Díaz
description: Leading academic hospital managed by Quirónsalud group, affiliated with Universidad Autónoma de Madrid. Its pathology department is recognized for translational research and molecular diagnostics.

name: Hospital Quirónsalud Barcelona
description: Flagship private hospital of the Quirónsalud group in Barcelona. ISO-certified pathology lab with state-of-the-art molecular diagnostics and international accreditation.

name: Hospital Universitario Quirónsalud Madrid
description: Major private university hospital in Madrid with JCI accreditation. Advanced pathology labs supporting a broad range of medical specialties with rapid diagnostic turnaround.

name: SYNLAB Anatomía Patológica Barcelona
description: SYNLAB's dedicated anatomical pathology laboratory in Barcelona. Provides biopsy processing, cytology, autopsies, and surgical specimen diagnosis with accredited high-technology processes.

name: SYNLAB Anatomía Patológica Madrid (Anapath)
description: SYNLAB's specialized anatomical pathology lab in Madrid (formerly Anapath S.L.). Processes biopsies, cytologies, and surgical specimens for hospitals and clinics across the Madrid region.

name: Hospital Universitario Puerta de Hierro Majadahonda
description: University hospital affiliated with Universidad Autónoma de Madrid. Known for transplant pathology and oncologic diagnostics with an active research program.

name: Hospital Universitario de Cruces
description: The largest hospital in the Basque Country affiliated with Universidad del País Vasco. Pathology department is a regional reference center for Northern Spain.

name: Hospital Universitario Reina Sofía
description: Major university hospital in Andalusia affiliated with Universidad de Córdoba. Its pathology department serves as a reference center for the province.

name: Centro Hospitalar Universitário de São João
description: The largest university hospital in Portugal affiliated with the Faculty of Medicine of the University of Porto. Major pathology department with extensive clinical and research output.

name: Hospital de Santa Maria (CHULN)
description: One of the largest general hospitals in Lisbon, part of Centro Hospitalar Universitário Lisboa Norte. Affiliated with University of Lisbon with a comprehensive Department of Pathology.

name: Instituto Português de Oncologia do Porto
description: National reference center for oncology serving 3.7 million people in Northern Portugal. OECI-accredited Comprehensive Cancer Center with a dedicated Service of Anatomical Pathology and tumor bank.

name: Instituto Português de Oncologia de Lisboa
description: Century-old comprehensive cancer center in Lisbon providing specialized anatomical pathology for cancer diagnosis. Part of the national IPO network with strong international research collaborations.

name: Instituto Português de Oncologia de Coimbra
description: Regional oncology referral center for central Portugal. Part of the national IPO network with integrated pathology and molecular research capabilities.

name: Centro Hospitalar e Universitário de Coimbra
description: Major university hospital affiliated with the University of Coimbra. The Institute of Anatomical & Molecular Pathology (IAP-PM), founded in 1956, is known for innovation in digital and experimental pathology.

name: Unilabs Portugal – Porto
description: Unilabs' main anatomical pathology hub in the Porto region. Operates multiple collection points across the metropolitan area supporting hospitals and clinics.

name: Unilabs Portugal – Lisboa
description: Unilabs' Lisbon-area anatomical pathology laboratory network. Provides diagnostic pathology services across the greater Lisbon metropolitan area.

name: Fondazione IRCCS Istituto Nazionale dei Tumori
description: Italy's premier National Tumor Institute and IRCCS-designated cancer center. International reference for rare and common cancers with a leading pathology department and extensive tumor biobank.

name: Istituto Europeo di Oncologia (IEO)
description: European Institute of Oncology, IRCCS-designated. Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine offers advanced diagnostics and clinical research in molecular and genomic pathology.

name: Fondazione IRCCS Ca' Granda Ospedale Maggiore Policlinico
description: One of Italy's oldest and most prestigious hospitals, IRCCS-designated. Affiliated with Università degli Studi di Milano with a renowned pathology department.

name: IRCCS Humanitas Research Hospital
description: IRCCS-designated research hospital led by Humanitas University. Anatomical pathology department specializes in oncological diagnostics with 24-72 hour turnaround and advanced molecular markers.

name: Policlinico di Sant'Orsola-Malpighi
description: Major university hospital affiliated with University of Bologna. Leading pathology and oncology research department in the Emilia-Romagna region.

name: Azienda Ospedaliero-Universitaria Careggi
description: The largest teaching hospital in Tuscany and a national center for pathology, oncology, and clinical research. Affiliated with Università degli Studi di Firenze.

name: Azienda Ospedale-Università di Padova
description: Major academic medical center affiliated with the University of Padua. Distinguished pathology department with strong research in oncologic and molecular pathology.

name: AOU Città della Salute e della Scienza di Torino (Molinette)
description: One of Europe's largest university hospital complexes, including the historic Molinette hospital. Comprehensive pathology services covering all subspecialties.

name: AOU Federico II
description: Major university hospital in Southern Italy affiliated with Università degli Studi di Napoli Federico II. Pathology department serves the Campania region.

name: IRCCS Fondazione G. Pascale – Istituto Nazionale Tumori Napoli
description: IRCCS-designated National Tumor Institute of Naples. Key center for cancer research and pathology in Southern Italy with strong molecular oncology capabilities.

name: AOU Policlinico di Bari
description: University hospital of Bari affiliated with Università degli Studi di Bari Aldo Moro. Main pathology reference center for the Puglia region.

name: AOU Policlinico G. Rodolico – San Marco Catania
description: Major university hospital in Sicily affiliated with Università degli Studi di Catania. Anatomical pathology department serves eastern Sicily.

name: Azienda Ospedaliero-Universitaria Pisana
description: University hospital affiliated with Università di Pisa. Known for endocrine pathology and thyroid cancer research with a strong molecular diagnostics program.

name: Istituto Oncologico Veneto (IOV) IRCCS
description: The only IRCCS-accredited cancer institute in the Veneto region. Multidisciplinary pathology department with integrated molecular diagnostics, partnered with the University of Padua.

name: Skåne University Hospital – Lund
description: Part of one of the world's largest digital pathology implementations. Region Skåne's four pathology labs (Lund, Malmö, Helsingborg, Kristianstad) produce 800,000+ slides/year, fully digital since 2019 using Sectra. Reporting times reduced by 27%.

name: Skåne University Hospital – Malmö
description: Malmö campus of Skåne University Hospital. Shares fully digital pathology workflow with Lund via Sectra platform. Part of the Region Skåne network producing 800,000+ histopathology slides per year.

name: Linköping University Hospital
description: University hospital serving southeastern Sweden (Östergötland). Houses a clinical pathology department with diagnostic histopathology, cytology, and molecular diagnostics. Affiliated with Linköping University.

name: Örebro University Hospital
description: Central Sweden's university hospital with a clinical pathology department providing histopathology, cytology, and molecular diagnostics. Affiliated with Örebro University.

name: Norrlands University Hospital (NUS)
description: Northern Sweden's primary university hospital, serving a vast geographic area. Pathology department provides diagnostic services for the entire Norrland region. Affiliated with Umeå University.

name: Sundsvalls sjukhus
description: Regional hospital in Västernorrland with a clinical pathology department. Serves the mid-Norrland region with histopathology and cytology diagnostics.

name: Länssjukhuset Ryhov
description: Main hospital in Jönköping County with a clinical pathology department providing histopathology and cytology services for the Småland highland region.

name: Centrallasarettet Växjö
description: Regional hospital in Kronoberg County with clinical pathology services including histopathology and cytology diagnostics.

name: Centralsjukhuset Karlstad
description: Värmland's main hospital with a clinical pathology department serving the region with histopathology, cytology, and immunohistochemistry.

name: Gävle sjukhus
description: Regional hospital in Gävleborg with a clinical pathology department providing diagnostic histopathology and cytology for the county.

name: Falu lasarett
description: Main hospital in Dalarna County with a clinical pathology laboratory providing histopathology and cytological diagnostics.

name: Länssjukhuset i Kalmar
description: Regional hospital in Kalmar County with clinical pathology services including diagnostic histopathology and cytology.

name: Helsingborgs lasarett
description: Part of Region Skåne's fully digital pathology network since 2019. One of four labs (Lund, Malmö, Helsingborg, Kristianstad) sharing the Sectra digital pathology platform.

name: Västmanlands sjukhus Västerås
description: Regional hospital in Västmanland with a clinical pathology department providing histopathology and cytology diagnostics.

name: Unilabs Skövde (Skaraborgs sjukhus)
description: Unilabs pathology laboratory at Skaraborgs sjukhus in Skövde. Provides clinical pathology and histopathology diagnostics for the Skaraborg area, operated by Unilabs Sweden.

name: Unilabs Eskilstuna (Mälarsjukhuset)
description: Unilabs pathology laboratory at Mälarsjukhuset in Eskilstuna. Provides histopathology and clinical pathology diagnostics for Sörmland County.

name: SYNLAB Sverige – Centrallaboratoriet Täby
description: SYNLAB Sweden's central pathology laboratory in Täby, Stockholm. One of Sweden's largest private lab providers with 12+ local sites in Stockholm and labs in Uppsala, Gothenburg, and Mölndal.

name: St. Olavs Hospital
description: University hospital for Central Norway (Helse Midt-Norge RHF). Pathology department provides diagnostic services for the Trøndelag region, affiliated with NTNU – Norwegian University of Science and Technology.

name: Stavanger University Hospital (SUS)
description: A leader in digital pathology and AI research in Norway. Active in the EU-funded Clarify Project for AI-driven digital pathology. Strong focus on breast cancer and colorectal cancer biomarker research.

name: Universitetssykehuset Nord-Norge (UNN)
description: The world's northernmost university hospital. Pathology department serves Northern Norway (Helse Nord RHF), covering a vast geographic area. Affiliated with UiT – The Arctic University of Norway.

name: Akershus University Hospital (Ahus)
description: One of Norway's largest acute hospitals by patient volume, serving the Akershus region east of Oslo. Pathology department handles diagnostic histopathology, cytology, and molecular pathology.

name: Sørlandet sykehus Kristiansand
description: Southern Norway's main hospital with a pathology department serving the Agder region. Part of Sørlandet Hospital Health Enterprise (SSHF).

name: Nordlandssykehuset Bodø
description: Hospital in Nordland County with a pathology department serving the Salten region of Northern Norway. Part of Helse Nord RHF.

name: Aarhus University Hospital (AUH)
description: Denmark's second-largest university hospital. Department of Histopathology provides diagnostic pathology for cancer and non-cancer diseases, with strong research and teaching programs.

name: Aalborg University Hospital
description: Main university hospital for North Jutland. Department of Pathology (Patologiafdelingen) provides diagnostic services, cancer research, and immunohistochemistry for the region.

name: Herlev Hospital
description: Major hospital in the Capital Region with pathology services, often collaborating with Rigshospitalet for advanced diagnostics. Part of Denmark's centralized diagnostic network contributing to the Danish Patobank.

name: Zealand University Hospital (Sjællands Universitetshospital)
description: University hospital for the Zealand region, with pathology and hematology departments supporting diagnostics across the region outside Copenhagen.

name: Turku University Hospital (TYKS)
description: TYKS Laboratories include departments for genomics, clinical chemistry, and pathology. Serves Turku and surrounding regions (Vakka-Suomi, Salo, Loimaa). Southwest Finland's primary pathology center.

name: Tampere University Hospital (TAYS) / Fimlab
description: Pathology and laboratory services for the Pirkanmaa (Tampere) region are provided by Fimlab Laboratories, supporting Tampere University Hospital with clinical pathology and molecular diagnostics.

name: Kuopio University Hospital (KYS) / ISLAB
description: Eastern Finland's university hospital with pathology diagnostics centralized under ISLAB. Serves North Savo and the broader eastern Finland area with histopathology and molecular pathology.

name: Oulu University Hospital (OYS) / Nordlab
description: Northern Finland's university hospital. Pathology and laboratory services managed by Nordlab, the principal lab provider for Oulu and partner districts in Northern Finland.

name: Landspítali – National University Hospital of Iceland
description: Iceland's only university hospital and principal pathology center. Handles all national anatomical pathology including surgical pathology, autopsies (clinical and forensic), and molecular pathology. Part of a modern laboratory centre with pneumatic tube transport. Affiliated with the University of Iceland.

name: Medical University of Warsaw – Department of Pathomorphology
description: University Center for Pathomorphology at Poland's leading medical university. Pioneer in digital pathology in Poland since 2014, with Hamamatsu NanoZoomer HT scanner for whole-slide imaging. Strong focus on digital teaching and AI-assisted diagnostics.

name: Jagiellonian University Medical College – Department of Pathomorphology
description: Collegium Medicum of the oldest university in Poland (founded 1364). Comprehensive pathology department serving the University Hospital in Kraków with strong academic and research traditions.

name: Medical University of Gdańsk – Department of Pathomorphology
description: Leading medical university in northern Poland with a well-established pathology department. Provides diagnostic services for the University Clinical Centre and engages in research and teaching.

name: Wrocław Medical University – Department of Pathomorphology
description: Major academic pathology center in Lower Silesia. The department provides comprehensive diagnostic and research services within the Wrocław university hospital system.

name: Poznań University of Medical Sciences – Department of Pathomorphology
description: One of Poland's leading medical schools with modern research and teaching facilities. Active pathology department engaged in advanced diagnostic techniques and interdisciplinary research.

name: Medical University of Łódź – Department of Pathomorphology
description: Large medical university in central Poland with a strong Department of Pathology. Active in clinical research and postgraduate education with emphasis on diagnostic excellence.

name: Medical University of Lublin – Department of Pathomorphology
description: Key academic pathology center in eastern Poland. Provides teaching, research, and clinical diagnostics. English-language medical program available for international students.

name: Medical University of Silesia – Department of Pathomorphology
description: Medical University of Silesia in Katowice serves the Upper Silesia industrial region. Major pathology department contributing to medical education and research in southern Poland.

name: Pomeranian Medical University in Szczecin – Department of Pathomorphology
description: Leading medical university in northwestern Poland. Comprehensive pathology curriculum and active research department supporting regional diagnostics.

name: Medical University of Białystok – Department of Pathomorphology
description: Academic pathology center in northeastern Poland. Recognized for research output in pathology and strong clinical diagnostics serving the Podlaskie region.

name: Maria Skłodowska-Curie National Research Institute of Oncology (NIO-PIB)
description: Poland's leading cancer center and OECI-accredited Comprehensive Cancer Center. Department of Pathology and Laboratory Diagnostics provides advanced histopathology, cytology, IHC, FISH, and NGS. Central role in multidisciplinary tumor boards and national cancer care guidelines.

name: Medicover Pathology Poland
description: Medicover's core digital histopathology hub in Łódź connects over 100 pathologists across Eastern Europe. Leading private pathology network with advanced whole-slide imaging, AI-assisted diagnostics, and second-opinion services for hospitals worldwide.

name: General University Hospital Prague – Institute of Pathology (1st Faculty of Medicine)
description: Institute of Pathology at the First Faculty of Medicine, Charles University and General University Hospital (VFN). Provides comprehensive diagnostic services including molecular diagnostics, immunohistochemistry, electron microscopy, and specialized subspecialties.

name: Motol University Hospital – Department of Pathology and Molecular Medicine (2nd Faculty of Medicine)
description: Department of Pathology and Molecular Medicine at one of the largest hospitals in Central Europe (80,000 inpatients, 800,000 outpatients/year). Key site for research and teaching within the Second Faculty of Medicine, Charles University.

name: Masaryk Memorial Cancer Institute (MMCI)
description: Czech Republic's only OECI-accredited National Oncology Center, established 1935. Pathology department specializes in histopathological and molecular diagnostics of cancer, including NGS. Operates RECAMO research center and accredited biobank.

name: University Hospital Olomouc – Institute of Clinical and Molecular Pathology
description: Affiliated with Palacký University Faculty of Medicine (since 1778). Comprehensive diagnostic services including biopsies, cytology, immunohistochemistry, molecular pathology, and electron microscopy. Participates in BBMRI.cz biobank network.

name: University Hospital Hradec Králové – Fingerland Department of Pathology
description: Internationally recognized pathology department affiliated with Charles University. Specialized in cancer predictive markers, CNS pathology, and advanced molecular diagnostics including NGS and tissue microarrays. Contributes to WHO tumor classification.

name: University Hospital Plzeň – Department of Pathology
description: Pathology department affiliated with the Faculty of Medicine in Plzeň, Charles University. Key academic and clinical center for western Bohemia providing diagnostic pathology, research, and medical education.

name: SYNLAB Czech Republic
description: Major private laboratory diagnostics provider in the Czech Republic, part of SYNLAB's European network. Offers broad pathology, clinical biochemistry, microbiology, and molecular genetics diagnostics for hospitals and clinics.

name: University Hospital Bratislava – Department of Pathological Anatomy
description: Slovakia's principal university hospital and birthplace of Slovak pathology tradition. Founded alongside Comenius University in 1919, the pathology department laid the foundations for pathology as a discipline across Slovakia.

name: Martin University Hospital – Department of Pathological Anatomy
description: Main teaching hospital of the Jessenius Faculty of Medicine, Comenius University. Department of Pathological Anatomy provides biopsy, cytology, and autopsy diagnostics with undergraduate and postgraduate teaching programs.

name: Louis Pasteur University Hospital Košice – Department of Pathology
description: One of Slovakia's largest hospitals with a pathology department dating back to 1948. Affiliated with Pavol Jozef Šafárik University. Provides comprehensive diagnostic and forensic pathology services for eastern Slovakia.

name: Semmelweis University – 1st Department of Pathology and Experimental Cancer Research
description: One of Europe's leading pathology centers within Hungary's largest healthcare institution. Bridges clinical diagnostics with experimental cancer research. Subspecialty services include breast pathology, dermatopathology, hematopathology, and multiomics research. Teaches in Hungarian, English, and German.

name: University of Debrecen – Department of Pathology
description: One of Hungary's largest pathology departments, operating across three campuses. Provides histopathology, cytology (including Pap and FNAC), molecular diagnostics (FISH, NGS), and autopsy services. Ranked #2 for pathology in Hungary.

name: University of Pécs – Department of Pathology
description: Department of Pathology at the University of Pécs Medical School. Operates specialized labs for aspiration cytology, flow cytometry, cancer cytogenetics, and molecular biology diagnostics. Strong teaching program in theoretical medicine.

name: University of Szeged – Department of Pathology
description: Part of the Faculty of General Medicine at the University of Szeged. Provides full-range diagnostic pathology services and participates actively in the medical curriculum. Ranked #3 for pathology in Hungary.

name: National Institute of Oncology (NIO) Budapest
description: Hungary's largest and most prominent cancer care institution. Only OECI-accredited Comprehensive Cancer Center in Hungary and Central/Eastern Europe. Works closely with Semmelweis University on multidisciplinary oncology care and leads European oncology projects.

name: Institutul Oncologic 'Prof. Dr. Alexandru Trestioreanu' (IOB)
description: Romania's largest oncology institute and national center for cancer diagnosis, treatment, and research. Comprehensive pathology laboratory (Anatomie Patologică) supports multidisciplinary care. Coordinator for the national pediatric oncology network.

name: Fundeni Clinical Institute – Department of Pathology
description: Major Romanian clinical institute renowned for complex pathology, organ transplants, and bone marrow transplants. Collaborates closely with IOB for comprehensive cancer care. Dedicated clinic for pediatric oncology and hematology.

name: Institutul Oncologic 'Prof. Dr. Ion Chiricuță' Cluj-Napoca (IOCN)
description: One of the first specialized cancer centers in Europe, established 1929. OECI member institution providing advanced pathology diagnostics, cancer treatments, and robust clinical research. Referral center for adult and pediatric oncology in Transylvania.

name: Victor Babeș University of Medicine and Pharmacy Timișoara – Department of Anatomical Pathology
description: Historic pathology department dating back to 1948. Comprehensive teaching and research in morphopathology. Organizes annual international conferences 'Pathology Days in Timișoara' (PATHOTIM) and postgraduate pathology courses.

name: Grigore T. Popa University of Medicine and Pharmacy Iași – Department of Pathology
description: Top-ranked Romanian institution for pathology. The department supports both clinical and academic training, is recognized for research excellence, and contributes to diagnostics, teaching, and pathology research at national and international levels.

name: Medicover Romania (Synevo) – Central Reference Laboratory
description: Key Medicover laboratory diagnostics provider in Romania operating through the Synevo brand. Central Reference Laboratory provides histopathology with digital scanners connected to Medicover's international expert network. Expanded in 2025 by acquiring SYNLAB Romania operations.

name: Medical University of Sofia – Alexandrovska University Hospital, Department of Pathology
description: Bulgaria's oldest university and multispecialty hospital, closely affiliated with the Medical University of Sofia. State-of-the-art laboratory and imaging equipment for pathology. Central base for undergraduate and postgraduate training in clinical pathology.

name: Medical University of Plovdiv – Department of General and Clinical Pathology
description: Key faculty component of Medical University of Plovdiv. Offers extensive teaching in general and clinical pathology to medical, dental, and pharmacy students with structured curriculum and research opportunities.

name: Medical University of Varna – Department of General and Clinical Pathology
description: Department within the Faculty of Medicine at Medical University of Varna. Provides undergraduate and postgraduate medical education in pathology for the Black Sea region of Bulgaria.

name: University Hospital Centre Zagreb (KBC Zagreb) – Clinical Department of Pathology and Cytology
description: Croatia's largest university hospital centre. Processes over 30,000 pathohistological samples annually. Advanced capabilities include PCR, flow cytometry, NGS, FISH, electron microscopy, and comprehensive cytology services across multiple locations (Rebro, Petrova, Šalata, Jordanovac).

name: University Hospital Centre Split (KBC Split) – Clinical Institute of Pathology, Forensic Medicine, and Cytology
description: Core pathology center for southern Croatia. Offers pathohistological diagnostics, molecular pathology including genetic tests (BRCA1/2, EGFR, KRAS, NRAS, BRAF, PIK3CA), cytological diagnostics, and forensic medicine services.

name: Clinical Hospital Centre Rijeka (KBC Rijeka) – Clinical Institute of Pathology and Cytology
description: Multidisciplinary university hospital serving as a base for medical, dental, and health science education at the University of Rijeka. Dedicated pathology institute as part of the diagnostic core.

name: University Hospital Centre Osijek (KBC Osijek) – Department of Pathology
description: University hospital in eastern Croatia affiliated with the Faculty of Medicine Osijek. Provides pathology diagnostics, research, and teaching for the Slavonia region.

name: University Clinical Centre of Serbia (UCCS) Belgrade – Institute of Pathology
description: Largest academic medical center in Serbia and one of the largest in Europe (3,150 beds, 34 hectares). Treats ~1 million patients annually. Main teaching hospital for University of Belgrade Faculty of Medicine. Renovated facilities completed 2022.

name: Clinical Center of Vojvodina – Center for Pathology and Histology
description: Major hospital providing tertiary healthcare for northern Serbia (Vojvodina). Center for Pathology and Histology conducts research and diagnostics. Closely associated with the University of Novi Sad medical faculty.

name: University Medical Centre Ljubljana – Institute of Pathology
description: Slovenia's oldest and leading pathology institution, established 1940. Employs 23 pathologists and 91 professional associates. Covers histopathology, cytopathology, molecular genetics, and immunopathology. Part of the Faculty of Medicine, University of Ljubljana.

name: University Medical Centre Maribor – Department of Pathology
description: Major healthcare institution with 1,316 beds, ~600 medical doctors, 60,000 inpatients and 390,000+ outpatients annually. Department of Pathology performs histological examinations, autopsies, and forensic examinations for northeastern Slovenia.

name: Clinical Center University of Sarajevo – Department of Pathology
description: Leading clinical institution in Bosnia and Herzegovina. Provides pathology diagnostics, medical education, and research. Hosts the Bryan Warren School of Pathology training events for regional pathologists.

name: Institute of Pathology, Faculty of Medicine, Ss. Cyril and Methodius University – Skopje
description: Primary pathology institution in North Macedonia, part of the Faculty of Medicine at the largest university. Active through the Macedonian Association of Pathology and the IAP division for organizing international congresses and educational events.

name: University Hospital Centre 'Mother Theresa' Tirana – Department of Pathology
description: Albania's principal referral and teaching hospital. Main pathology institution in the country providing clinical diagnostics and academic training for medical students and pathology specialists.

name: Clinical Center of Montenegro – Department of Pathology
description: Central pathology institution in Montenegro. Provides pathology diagnostic services and participates in regional academic activities and continuing education forums for Balkan pathologists.

name: Nicolae Testemițanu State University of Medicine and Pharmacy – Department of Pathology
description: Moldova's premier medical school and primary institution for pathology education and diagnostics. Department integrates pathology into clinical and laboratory medicine training. Connected to Medicover's digital pathology network.

name: University Clinical Centre of Kosovo – Institute of Pathology, University of Prishtina
description: Leading institution for medical education and pathology in Kosovo. The Department of Pathology at the University of Prishtina provides diagnostic and educational services as the country's primary academic pathology center.

name: Evangelismos General Hospital Athens – Department of Pathology
description: Greece's largest general and teaching hospital, affiliated with the National and Kapodistrian University of Athens. Its anatomical pathology department is a national referral center for complex cases, offering histology, cytology, immunohistochemistry, and molecular diagnostics.

name: AHEPA University General Hospital of Thessaloniki – Laboratory of Pathology
description: 680-bed tertiary referral center for Northern Greece affiliated with Aristotle University. The Laboratory of General Pathology & Pathological Anatomy handles tissue biopsies, cytology, and autopsies supporting both clinical diagnostics and academic research.

name: University General Hospital of Heraklion (PAGNI) – Pathology Department
description: Largest public hospital in Crete (750 beds), affiliated with the University of Crete School of Medicine. Modernized and digitalized pathology laboratory supporting both clinical diagnostics and research, serving all of southern Greece.

name: Attikon University General Hospital – Department of Pathological Anatomy
network: National and Kapodistrian University of Athens
description: Major academic teaching hospital in western Athens affiliated with the National and Kapodistrian University of Athens Medical School. Active pathological anatomy department providing diagnostic services and contributing to clinical and translational research.

name: Hippokration General Hospital of Athens – Pathology Department
description: One of Athens' largest and most historic public hospitals (est. 1922) with 476 beds. University-affiliated with the National and Kapodistrian University of Athens, providing comprehensive pathology diagnostic services for central Athens.

name: University Hospital of Ioannina – Department of Pathology
description: Largest hospital in western Greece (845 beds) affiliated with the University of Ioannina. The pathology department, headed by Prof. Anna Batistatou, is internationally recognized for its courses in pathology organized under the European Society of Pathology.

name: General University Hospital of Patras – Department of Pathology
description: Major 800-bed teaching hospital affiliated with the University of Patras Medical School. The pathology department provides diagnostic histopathological, cytological, and molecular services for the southwestern Greek region with strong research in tumor mechanisms.

name: Papageorgiou General Hospital Thessaloniki – Pathology Department
description: Modern 800-bed hospital affiliated with Aristotle University featuring ISO 9001, EN 15224, and ISO 15189 certifications. Houses a Molecular Oncology Laboratory and clinical studies unit with strong emphasis on quality management.

name: Bioiatriki Healthcare Group – Central Pathology Laboratory
description: Greece's largest private diagnostic network with 70+ centers across Greece and Cyprus, serving 3M+ patients annually. ISO 15189 and ISO 9001 accredited central labs perform 3,500+ test types with fully automated workflows and CAP quality control participation.

name: IASO Hospital Athens – Pathology Laboratory
description: Leading private hospital in Athens with 8 anatomic pathologists. The pathology lab features digital specimen recording, immunohistochemistry with HER2/Ki-67/PD-L1 testing, and participates in external quality assessments. ISO 15189 accredited molecular lab performs 1M+ tests annually.

name: Hacettepe University Hospitals – Department of Pathology
description: Established in 1963, one of Turkey's most prestigious pathology departments serving Adult, Oncology, and Children's Hospitals. Features a dedicated Philips-supported digital pathology platform for education and Leica Aperio scanning capability.

name: Istanbul University-Cerrahpaşa – Cerrahpaşa Faculty of Medicine, Department of Pathology
description: Turkey's first modern medical school (est. 1827). The pathology department at Cerrahpaşa specializes in both diagnostic and research activities including surgical pathology, cytopathology, and autopsy services with advanced immunohistochemistry and molecular pathology labs.

name: Istanbul University – Istanbul Faculty of Medicine (Çapa), Department of Pathology
description: One of Turkey's oldest and most prestigious medical faculties (Çapa campus). The pathology department has a strong academic tradition in diagnostics, education, and research with numerous international publications in cancer pathology.

name: Ege University Faculty of Medicine – Department of Medical Pathology
description: Established in the late 1950s, Ege's pathology department has robust infrastructure including histochemistry, immunofluorescence, and electron microscopy. Pioneers in organ-system based sub-specialization within pathology in Turkey, with over 100 trained pathology experts.

name: Dokuz Eylül University Hospital – Department of Pathology
description: Prominent pathology department located at the 15 Temmuz Health Campus in Balçova. Collaborates with the İzmir Biomedicine and Genome Center (iBG) for translational and genomic medicine research. Strong team covering all subspecialties.

name: Ankara University Faculty of Medicine – Department of Pathology
description: Part of Turkey's top-ranked medical faculty (est. 1945). The pathology department operates across the Morphology Campus and Cebeci Hospital, contributing to clinical diagnostics, graduate/doctoral training, and interdisciplinary research in tumor biology and stem cell science.

name: Gazi University Hospital – Department of Medical Pathology
description: Active since 1980, processes ~34,000 biopsies, 13,500 cytology cases, and 1,100 frozen sections annually. Equipped with EGFR/KRAS/BRAF PCR-based genotyping, FISH/SISH, electron microscopy, and comprehensive immunohistochemistry capabilities.

name: Marmara University School of Medicine – Department of Medical Pathology
description: Located at Başıbüyük Campus in Maltepe, Istanbul. Features dedicated histopathology, cytopathology, immunohistochemistry, and molecular pathology laboratories. First Turkish university to offer a Pathology Laboratory Techniques program (1991-92).

name: Başkent University Ankara Hospital – Department of Pathology
description: Part of the Başkent University Healthcare Group operating 10 hospitals across Turkey. The pathology department provides diagnostic and educational services, supporting the group's renowned organ transplantation and oncology programs.

name: Koç University Hospital – Department of Pathology
description: Established 2014, JCI-accredited hospital with ~15 expert pathologists. Features 300+ immunohistochemistry antibodies, next-generation sequencing, real-time PCR, electron microscopy, and liquid biopsy capabilities. Leading referral center performing ~1,500 molecular tests/year.

name: Acıbadem Healthcare Group – Central Pathology Laboratory
description: Turkey's first ISO 15189-accredited clinical laboratory network. Central 540m² pathology lab at Acıbadem University processes ~50,000 biopsies and 60,000 cytology samples monthly. Features a telepathology system connecting 11 hospitals for remote consultations.

name: Memorial Hospitals Group – Pathology Department
description: First private hospital in Turkey with JCI accreditation. Central pathology labs in Istanbul and Antalya with FDA-approved digital pathology infrastructure. Team of 12 specialist physicians serving 11 facilities and 75,000+ patients annually with data-matrix tracking at every stage.

name: SYNLAB Turkey (Synevo/Medicover)
description: International laboratory network operating in Turkey under the Synevo and Medicover brands. Headquarters in Ankara with major facilities in Istanbul (Mecidiyeköy) and İzmir, providing pathology and cytology services alongside comprehensive clinical diagnostics.

name: Tartu University Hospital – Pathology Department
description: Estonia's largest and only academic hospital. In 2025, implemented the country's first integrated pathology information system (with Nortal), connecting 5 hospitals. Features automated cassette/slide printing saving 31,600+ sheets/year, with AI-ready scalable architecture.

name: North Estonia Medical Centre (PERH) – Pathology Laboratory
description: Estonia's premier tertiary medical facility, ISO 15189 accredited (Certificate M012). Among the largest medical labs in Estonia, handling high-complexity cases including cancer and hematological disorders. Located at the Mustamäe campus in Tallinn.

name: Pauls Stradiņš Clinical University Hospital – Pathology Department
network: Riga Stradiņš University / University of Latvia
description: One of Latvia's largest and most important healthcare institutions, affiliated with both the University of Latvia and Riga Stradiņš University. Recent infrastructure investments include new pathology laboratory information systems in the A2 building. Covers 26+ medical specialties.

name: Riga East University Hospital – Pathology Center
description: Houses the dedicated Pathology Center at Hipokrāta Street 2 and operates the Oncology Centre of Latvia. The Academic Histology Laboratory is Latvia's largest outpatient pathology analysis provider. Active in European cancer research projects and biobank development.

name: Vilnius University Hospital Santaros Klinikos / National Center of Pathology
description: Lithuania's National Center of Pathology (est. 1979), the Baltic region's largest pathology institution. Processes ~48,000 surgical pathology cases, ~65,000 cytopathology tests, and ~100 autopsies annually. Serves hospitals across Lithuania and internationally (Latvia, Kyrgyzstan).

name: LSMU Kaunas Hospital (Kauno Klinikos) – Pathology Department
description: Lithuania's largest healthcare center integrating teaching, diagnostics, and research. The pathology department at Eivenių Str. 2 is led by Dr. Lina Poškienė and supports both clinical care and scientific research as the primary teaching hospital for LSMU.

name: Nicosia General Hospital – Pathology / Histopathology Department
description: Cyprus's central reference pathology center, serving all SHSO public hospitals and the Bank of Cyprus Oncology Centre. Features 300+ immunohistochemistry antibodies, FISH, direct immunofluorescence, and Next-Generation Sequencing for solid tumors. Participates in national external quality assessments.

name: Limassol General Hospital – Pathology Department
description: Largest hospital in the Limassol district providing secondary and tertiary healthcare. Offers histopathology, cytology, and immunohistochemistry services. Supported by specialized external histopathology centers in Limassol for advanced diagnostics.

name: Mater Dei Hospital – Pathology Department / Cellular Pathology Laboratory
description: Malta's main acute general teaching hospital affiliated with the University of Malta. The Cellular Pathology Laboratory has undergone significant EU-funded digital transformation with high-definition slide scanners. Offers 1,500-1,700+ test types with 24/7 urgent testing capability.

name: National Cancer Institute – Pathology Department
description: Ukraine's premier state oncology center providing multidisciplinary cancer care, research, and education. The pathology department features modern diagnostic capabilities for evidence-based cancer diagnosis. Staff participates in international congresses and develops clinical guidelines for Ukraine.

name: Bogomolets National Medical University – Department of Pathological Anatomy
description: One of Ukraine's oldest medical institutions (est. 1841). The Department of Pathological Anatomy is among the oldest in Eastern Europe, providing teaching, research, and clinical consulting in collaboration with the Kyiv City Clinical Oncology Center. WHO/UNESCO recognized.

name: Kyiv City Clinical Oncology Center – Department of Pathology
description: Major oncology center closely affiliated with the National Cancer Institute and Bogomolets NMU. Pathologists hold the highest qualification category in 'Pathological anatomy', actively participate in international congresses, and contribute to Ministry of Health clinical guidelines.

name: Danylo Halytsky Lviv National Medical University – Department of Pathological Anatomy
description: One of Ukraine's oldest (est. 1784) and top-3 ranked medical universities. The Department of Pathological Anatomy and Forensic Medicine is headed by Prof. Yurii Pospishil. Active in research including retrospective COVID-19 autopsy analysis. Offers education in English and Ukrainian.

name: Kharkiv National Medical University – Department of General and Clinical Pathological Physiology
description: Established in 1871, one of Ukraine's oldest medical universities. The pathology department focuses on inflammation, blood system pathology, and pharmacological research including stem cell applications. Active Pathology Student Scientific Society fosters research development.

name: Odesa National Medical University – Department of Pathological Anatomy
description: Ranked #1 in Odesa and #12 in Ukraine for pathology research and education. The pathology department offers courses in multiple languages (English, Ukrainian, Russian) and is recognized internationally (WHO, ECFMG, GMC, NMC). Features simulation labs and modern medical equipment.
